text:
Shane Ó Curraighín

North West Correspondent

Donegal and Sligo athletics clubs will celebrate their Olympic stars this week.

Raphoe Badminton Club will host a homecoming for badminton star Rachael Darragh at the Diamond in Raphoe on Friday at 8pm.

In a social media post, Raphoe Badminton Club said: "Rachael played her heart out in Paris and as a community, town and club we are all so proud of everything she has achieved over the last three years to qualify for the Olympic Games."

Finn Valley AC will host a homecoming reception for 800m semi-finalist Mark English at 2pm on Saturday. The club has extended an open invitation for everyone to attend.

Three Donegal Athletes contributed to Team Ireland's Olympic campaign. An event for 400m relay runner Kelly McGrory (Tir Chonaill AC) has not yet been confirmed as she has work commitments, but details will be announced as soon as she returns to the county.

Sligo had four members on Team Ireland in Paris. Mona McSharry, who won bronze in the Women's 100m Breaststroke; boxer Dean Clancy (Sean McDermott Boxing Club) who made his Olympics debut in the 63.5kg weight division as well as Chris O'Donnell (North Sligo AC) and Lauren Cadden (Sligo AC) who were part of the Irish Mixed 4x400m team.

Sligo County Council is understood to be planning a civic reception for the county's four Olympians later in the autumn.

A homecoming is planned for Olympic medalist Mona McSharry in her hometown of Grange in the next few weeks.

In Grange, Co Sligo, North Sligo Athletics Club held a celebration for local hero Christopher O'Donnell, a two-time Olympian and recent European gold medalist.

Christopher was a member of the Irish mixed 4x400m relay team.

North West Correspondent

title: Local homecomings for north west Olympic stars
author: Shane Ó Curraighín
description: Donegal and Sligo athletics clubs will celebrate their Olympic stars this week.
